metaTitle: Lost something in a Seoul taxi — recovery service | LFK
metaDescription: Left your phone, wallet, or bag in a Seoul taxi? We track it down through Korean taxi networks and ship it home.
h1: Lost something in a Seoul taxi
heroBody: Phones in the back seat, bags in the trunk, jackets pinned between the seat and the door — taxis are one of the most common places travelers lose things in Seoul. The good news: Korean taxi drivers usually turn items in to a police box within a day. The hard part is identifying which taxi, and that's where the receipt or driver number matters.

steps.title: Find your taxi receipt or driver info
steps.body: Check your wallet, jacket pocket, or your card statement for a paper receipt — it has the driver's license number, taxi number, and time. If you paid by card or T-money, the transaction record helps us identify the taxi company. App-hailed rides (Kakao T, Uber) keep digital records.

steps.title: Submit a free report with what you have
steps.body: Tell us: pickup and drop-off locations, approximate time, item description, and any driver/taxi info from your receipt or app. Even partial info — like 'paid by Visa around 9pm in Hongdae' — narrows the search significantly.

steps.title: We work the taxi networks in Korean
steps.body: Major Seoul taxi companies, Kakao T's lost-and-found team, and the relevant police boxes — we call all of them in Korean and check the Lost112 national database. Items typically surface within 24–72 hours.

steps.title: Retrieve and ship
steps.body: Once located, we collect the item from wherever it ended up (police box, taxi company office, or the driver directly), package it, and ship via EMS to your address.

faqs.q: I didn't keep the receipt — is it hopeless?
faqs.a: No, just harder. We can still check Lost112 and the major taxi networks against your description. The match rate is lower but recoveries do happen, especially for distinctive items.

faqs.q: I used Kakao T / a ride-hailing app
faqs.a: Easier — Kakao T has a structured lost-and-found process. We contact them on your behalf with your trip ID; they reach the driver. Most app-hailed cases resolve within 24 hours.

faqs.q: What if the driver kept it?
faqs.a: Rare — Korean taxi drivers have a strong cultural and legal incentive to turn items in (they get a small finder's reward from the city, and not turning items in is a license issue). When it does happen, the receipt info gives us the driver's name and we escalate.

faqs.q: Can I get cash back if my wallet was in the cab?
faqs.a: Cash is the most commonly missing item when wallets surface. Cards, IDs, and the wallet itself almost always come back intact.

internalLinks.label: Lost something on the Seoul subway
